question_text: Which of the following is an equity value multiple (not an enterprise value multiple)?
explanation: Price/Earnings (P/E) is an equity value multiple because the numerator (share price or market cap) represents equity value, and the denominator (net income or EPS) is a metric available only to equity holders (it is after interest expense and taxes). The rule for matching: Enterprise Value multiples use metrics available to all capital providers (pre-interest: Revenue, EBITDA, EBIT), while Equity Value multiples use metrics available only to equity holders (post-interest: Net Income, EPS, Free Cash Flow to Equity). Mixing them (e.g., EV/Net Income) creates a mismatch and produces meaningless results.
